title: The substitution
subtitle: How the public library became a “user”
caption: Word frequency (occurrences per 10,000 words) in the ILA proceedings, from the terms' verified anchor readings. Public library (red) falls from its planning-era peak of 26.8 (1950s–60s) to 4.1 by 2000–08; user climbs from 13.0 (1970–85) to 47.1 (2000–08); digital rises from near-absence to 41.7 (2009–19), the one term still climbing at the end of the series. (Information runs off the top of this scale, peaking at 136.)

data.label: public library
data.note: A public claim on knowledge, collapsing.

data.label: user
data.note: The reader, recoded as a function of a system.

data.label: digital
data.note: A connection offered where a commons was owed. Still rising at the end of the series.
